zh.lifeSummary: Alice de Buci於大約1075年出生在英國斯塔福爾德雷頓 巴塞特。她於1098年與Sir Ralph Basset Justicar在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾德雷頓 巴塞特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1154年在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾德雷頓 巴塞特享年81歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾德雷頓 巴塞特聖 比特也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Alice de Buci于大约1075年出生在英国斯塔福爾德雷頓 巴塞特。她于1098年与Sir Ralph Basset Justicar在英国英格兰斯塔福爾德雷頓 巴塞特结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1154年在英国英格兰斯塔福爾德雷頓 巴塞特享年81岁，埋葬在英国斯塔福爾德雷頓 巴塞特聖 比特也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Buci
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English buc(ke) ‘male goat’ (Old English bucca) or a ‘male deer’ (Old English bucc). The goat was popularly associated with lecherous behaviour and the deer with timidity and speed. The surname may also be a shortened form of longer occupational names, for example Roger le Bucmanger' ‘dealer in bucks or venison’, (Warwickshire 1221) or Walter Bucswayn perhaps ‘goatherd’ (Somerset 1327).
English: topographic name for someone who lived near a prominent beech tree, such as Peter atte Buk (Suffolk 1327), from Middle English buk ‘beech’ (from Old English bōc).
German and Dutch (Limburg): from a personal name, a short form of Burkhard (see Burkhart ).

variants.name: Buckson
meanings: English: either a patronymic from   Buck  , orpossibly an altered form of   Buxton  .

variants.name: Burkhart
meanings: German: from the medieval personal name Burkhard, from ancient Germanic Burghard, composed of the elements burg ‘fort, castle’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. This form of the surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and in Czechia (compare 2 below), but it is very rare there. Compare also Borgardt , Brookhart , Burgert , Burghart , Burkart , and Burkett .
Germanized form of Polish and Czech Burkert , Czech Purkert or Purkart, and Slovenian Purkart: from a German variant and Slavicized forms of the medieval German personal name Burkhard (see 1 above).

variants.name: Buxton
meanings: English:

habitational name from Buxton in Derbyshire, which in Middle English was called Buchestanes, Bucstones (i.e. ‘bowing stones’, from Middle English b(o)ugen, Old English būgan ‘to bow’ + stanes ‘stones’). It is probably named for logan stones in the vicinity. (Logan stones are boulders so poised that they rock at a touch.)
less commonly, a habitational name from Buxton in Norfolk, which is named with the genitive case of the Old English personal name Bucc (see Buck 1) + Old English tūn ‘settlement, enclosure’.

variants.name: Buckman
meanings: English: occupational name for a goatherd, Middle English bukkeman (from Old English bucca ‘he-goat’ + mann ‘man’).
English: occupational name for a scholar, scribe student, or bookbinder, from Middle English bok, buk(e) ‘book’ + man (from Old English bōc ‘book’ + mann ‘man’).
Americanized form of German Buckmann or Bückmann: derivative of Buck .

variants.name: Boughton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places so named. Those in Cambridgeshire (formerly Huntingdonshire), Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Northamptonshire, and Nottinghamshire are named from Old English bucc ‘goat’ or the Old English byname Bucca with the same meaning (see Buck 1) + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’; those in Cheshire and Kent are named with Old English bōc ‘beech’ + tūn.

variants.name: Buckmaster
meanings: English (southeastern): habitational name from Buckminster in Leicestershire, named with the Old English personal name Bucca (see Buck ) + Old English mynster ‘minster, large church’.

variants.name: Bucknell
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places called Bucknell in Oxfordshire and Shropshire, Bucknall in Lincolnshire and Somerset, or Bucknowl in Dorset. These are all named with the Old English byname Bucca (see Buck ) or Old English bucca ‘he-goat’ (with genitive -n) + hyll ‘hill’ in the first two examples or healh ‘nook, hollow’ in the latter two. Bucknowl in Dorset, recorded as Bubecnolle in 1285, is named from the Old English personal name Bubba (see Bubb ) + cnoll ‘hill top’.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

variants.name: Alis
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Alice de Buci was born about 1075, in Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England. She married Sir Ralph Basset Justicar in 1098, in Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. She died about 1154, in Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 81, and was buried in St Peter's Church, Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Alice de Buci est née environ 1075, à Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Sir Ralph Basset Justicar en 1098, à Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1154, à Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 81 ans, et a été enterrée à St Peter's Church, Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

Sie hat 1098 in Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Sir Ralph Basset Justicar geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. it.lifeSummary: Alice de Buci è nata il circa 1075 nel Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England. Lei sposato Sir Ralph Basset Justicar nel 1098, nel Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì circa 1154, nel Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 81 anni, e fu sepolta a St Peter's Church, Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice de Buciは約1075年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアドレイトン バセットで生まれた。1098年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアドレイトン バセットでSir Ralph Basset Justicarと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1154年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアドレイトン バセットで81歳で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアドレイトン バセットスト ペテル'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St Peter's Church, Drayton Bassett, Staf: 영국 스태퍼드실르 드라이턴 배싯 스트 피터'스 처르치
ko.Abingdon-on-Thames, Vale of White Horse,: 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 발 프 휘트 호르스
ko.lifeSummary: Alice de Buci(은)는 대략 1075년 영국 스태퍼드실르 드라이턴 배싯에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1098년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 드라이턴 배싯에서 Sir Ralph Basset Justicar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1154년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 드라이턴 배싯에서 81세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 드라이턴 배싯 스트 피터'스 처르치에 묻혔다.

ru.Abingdon-on-Thames, Vale of White Horse,: веил оф уит хаурс, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Alice de Buci родилась около 1075 г. в местности дреитон бассетт, стаффордшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Ralph Basset Justicar в 1098 г. в местности дреитон бассетт, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Она умерла около 1154 г. в местности дреитон бассетт, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 81 год и была похоронена в местности ст питир'с чурч, дреитон бассетт, стаффордшир, Англия.
